title: Financial Stability Report; November 2025

source.name: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System

summary: The Federal Reserve Board released its November 2025 Financial Stability Report, assessing four key vulnerability categories: asset valuations, borrowing by businesses and households, leverage in the financial sector, and funding risks. The report, based on data available as of October 23, 2025, includes a dedicated box on AI and algorithmic trading and a targeted assessment of short-term funding risk. The framework distinguishes between difficult-to-predict shocks and monitorable vulnerabilities that can amplify stress when the financial system is hit by adverse events.
key_points: Report assesses four vulnerability categories: valuation pressures, excessive borrowing, financial sector leverage, and funding risks
Data reflects information available as of October 23, 2025 (approximately 5 months prior to TODAY)
Funding risks section includes a targeted assessment of short-term funding risk (Box 4.1)
Box 1.1 covers artificial intelligence and algorithmic trading as a specific assessment area
Report explicitly covers liquidity and maturity transformation risks where institutions invest short-term funds in illiquid or long-maturity assets
Framework emphasizes how vulnerabilities can interact to amplify systemic stress
why_it_matters: This semi-annual report provides the Federal Reserve's official assessment of U.S. financial system resilience. For market structure analysis, it directly addresses funding liquidity risks, the functioning of short-term funding markets, and the potential for fire sale dynamics. The AI/algorithmic trading box suggests growing regulatory attention to automated market dynamics. Financial stability assessments inform supervisory priorities and potential regulatory responses that affect market structure.
macro_context: The report focuses on financial system resilience rather than macroeconomic conditions. It does not provide new forecasts or policy rate guidance but rather assesses the build-up or reduction of vulnerabilities in the U.S. financial system.
market_structure_context: Directly relevant to market structure. The report addresses: (1) liquidity and maturity transformation risks in short-term funding markets, (2) fire sale dynamics when institutions are forced to sell assets quickly, (3) interconnectedness across markets and institutions, and (4) the role of AI and algorithmic trading in market functioning. The funding risks section specifically examines institutions raising funds with short-notice commitments while investing in hard-to-sell or long-maturity assets.
